item_name: Argentina: ANMAT registration timelines (generics and biologics)
access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (ANMAT registration is gatekeeper to all formal procurement channels - PAMI, obras sociales, BNDE, private). Registration delays push patients toward judicial (amparo de salud) channel for not-yet-approved or pending drugs - 30.6% of judicialized drugs in 2017-2020 were unregistered in Argentina.
procurement_channels: Not procurement directly; but ANMAT registration is mandatory precondition for all PAMI tender, obras sociales formulary, BNDE and private procurement. ANMAT also approves import authorizations under specific procedures.
key_actors: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica) - regulatory authority created Decree 1490/1992; INAME (Instituto Nacional de Medicamentos) within ANMAT - drug evaluation arm; INPI (Instituto Nacional de la Propiedad Industrial) - patents; CONETEC (Comision Nacional de Evaluacion de Tecnologias de Salud) - HTA created Res 623/2018, integrated into ANMAT scope; Ministry of Health (parent ministry).
formulary_coverage: Not applicable - ANMAT determines registration not formulary inclusion; but registration is precondition for cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin and trastuzumab inclusion in any formulary.
judicial_access_volume: Indirect impact: 30.6% of amparo-litigated drugs in Argentina (2017-2020) were not yet ANMAT-approved at time of court ruling - registration backlog/non-registration is a primary amparo driver. 405 amparo cases in 4-year period mainly involved high-priced drugs (Romero et al., Medicina B Aires 2024).
registration_timeline_generic: 12-18 months for small molecules / generics with bioequivalence demonstration (Artixio, 2024 industry summary). Generic application requires bioequivalence vs. ANMAT-approved reference. Provision 3752/2025 (May 29, 2025) reformed two-stage 45-business-day evaluation that previously totaled >4 months pure ANMAT review + production/RFI delays.
registration_timeline_biologic: 24 months for vaccines and biologicals (full dossier) per industry summary; biosimilars under Disposition 7729/2011 + Disposition 1741/2025 (comparability framework) follow abbreviated pathway with PK/PD/efficacy/immunogenicity/safety; abbreviated biosimilar route generally 18-24 months. [uncertain: average biosimilar review time post-2025 reform]
registration_authority: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica). Legal basis: Decree 1490/1992; reports to Ministry of Health of the Nation. Headquarters Buenos Aires.
biosimilar_pathway: Two-track pathway: (1) Disposition 7075/2011 - registration of biological products with no prior approved comparator; (2) Disposition 7729/2011 - registration of biosimilars referencing previously approved biological. Updated by Disposition 1741/2025 establishing detailed Comparability Guide aligned with EMA model: physicochemical analytics, non-clinical, clinical (PK, PD where applicable, efficacy if required, immunogenicity, overall safety). Reference product must be authorized and widely marketed in Argentina or in country with similar regulatory functions. Approved biosimilars include rituximab (since 2017), bevacizumab; trastuzumab biosimilars approved [uncertain: exact count and dates].
registration_backlog: Documented historic backlog driving 2025 reform package. ANMAT Regulations 4053/2025, 4054/2025, 4055/2025 eliminated/consolidated 300+ administrative procedures. Provision 3752/2025 streamlined commercialization approval. Specific number of pending dossiers not publicly published. [uncertain: precise # of pending registrations 2025]
reform_status: enacted: Provision 3752/2025 (May 29 2025) drug approval streamlining; enacted: ANMAT Regulations 4053/2025-4055/2025 procedural elimination; enacted: Disposition 1741/2025 biosimilar comparability framework; enacted: Decree 1741/2025 promoting local biosimilar production; potentially enacted: ANMAT Resolution 754/2025 mandatory shortage notification regime [uncertain - not directly verified in searches]; in_progress: international harmonization with FDA/EMA references.
currency_mechanism: Not directly relevant to ANMAT statutory function; however ANMAT fees and import authorizations interact with cepo cambiario / SIRA / SEDI regimes; pharmaceutical imports were given priority access (30-day payment) under Milei deregulation.
pharma_import_cost_impact: ANMAT does not set prices, but registration delays compound currency-driven import cost shocks - delays force payers to procure from registered alternatives often at higher prices.
financial_mechanism: ANMAT funded by registration fees + Treasury transfers; not a payer.
shortage_rate: Not applicable to ANMAT statutory data; ANMAT 2025 shortage notification reform [uncertain] should produce future numbers.
shortage_causes: Registration delays / backlog historically a contributor: drugs not registered cannot be procured formally and force amparo route. 2025 reforms specifically aim to reduce this lead-time as shortage cause.
shortage_monitoring_system: ANMAT operates pharmacovigilance system (SNFVG); 2025 reform package reportedly introducing mandatory MA-holder notification of shortages within set timeframe (Resolution 754/2025) [uncertain - not directly verified in searches]. Reform aligns with international shortage notification standards (FDA, EMA models).
oncology_specific_shortage_events: Not directly tracked at ANMAT level for Argentina-specific oncology agents. Cisplatin/carboplatin global shortage 2023-2024 (started Feb 2023, lasted to June 2024) impacted Argentina via supply chain dependence; ANMAT issued vigilance notices but no shortage list comparable to FDA published. [uncertain: ANMAT-published shortage list events]
source_citation: 1) ANMAT Disposition 7075/2011 - biological registration; 2) ANMAT Disposition 7729/2011 - biosimilar registration; 3) ANMAT Disposition 1741/2025 - comparability guide for biosimilars (also Decree 1741/2025 promoting local biosimilar production); 4) ANMAT Provision 3752/2025 (Boletin Oficial 29 May 2025) - drug approval streamlining; 5) ANMAT Regulations 4053/2025, 4054/2025, 4055/2025 - administrative procedure consolidation; 6) GaBi Online - Argentinian guidelines for similar biological medicines; 7) GaBi Online - Regulating biosimilars in Argentina: ANMAT's approach; 8) Artixio - Pharmaceutical Regulations and Registration in Argentina (2024); 9) PharmaRegulatory.in - ANMAT Drug Approval Guide; 10) Lexology - 2025 Summary of main regulatory updates Argentina; 11) Romero et al., Judicializacion de medicamentos de alto precio en Argentina, Medicina B Aires 2024; 12) Decree 1490/1992 - ANMAT creation.
source_type_granular: government_primary (ANMAT dispositions/regulations) + legal_regulatory_tracker (Lexology, Beccar Varela) + industry_association_FIFARMA + academic_peer_reviewed (Romero 2024 Medicina B Aires)
data_reliability: high for legal/regulatory text; medium for timeline averages (industry estimates rather than published ANMAT statistics)
uncertain: average biosimilar review time post-2025 reform
exact count and dates of trastuzumab biosimilars approved
ANMAT Resolution 754/2025 shortage notification regime confirmation
